turns.response_text: Searching vault… Searching vault (parallel)… Looking up episode… Searching transcripts… Searching vault… Searching transcripts… Searching vault… Searching transcripts… Honest answer: the vault doesn't cover Satya Nadella. I ran multiple search angles — his name directly, *Hit Refresh*, his major acquisitions (Minecraft, LinkedIn, GitHub), his cultural transformation at Microsoft, and broader queries about Microsoft acquisition strategy. None returned any Nadella-specific evidence. The list_episode_ids call for "Satya Nadella" returned zero results. What the vault *does* cover extensively is the Bill Gates era (ep-0044, ep-0140, ep-0174) and the founding of Microsoft, plus plenty on acquisition strategy from other founders — Rockefeller's stealth roll-ups, Billy Durant's indigestion at GM, Ben Horowitz's rules about when to sell, Henry Singleton's Teledyne approach. But Nadella's chapter at Microsoft isn't here. If you *know* you wrote notes about Nadella's acquisition approach — specific deal rationales, the cultural-fit lens, how he left acquired companies autonomous (LinkedIn, GitHub, Minecraft) versus the old Microsoft embrace-and-extinguish playbook — those notes may not have made it into the expanded vault yet. If you can point me to a specific episode where you discussed him, I can load it directly.
